level_signals: 4–6+ years in Sales Operations, Revenue Operations, or GTM Strategy
Reports to VP of Revenue Operations
Leads or co-leads weekly forecast calls with Sales leadership
Influences senior stakeholders without formal authority

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4–6+ years in Sales Operations, Revenue Operations, or GTM Strategy.
Experience supporting Direct Sales Teams and/or Channel organizations.
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to turn data into decisions.
Comfortable working with ambiguity and building structure where none exists.
Excellent executive communication — written, verbal, and storytelling.
Ability to influence senior stakeholders without formal authority.
High ownership mentality: you see problems and fix them.
Detail-oriented but able to zoom out to the bigger picture.
Strong experience with Salesforce, Tableau, Outreach, Clay, Scratchpad, and ZoomInfo.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Familiarity working alongside tools like Anaplan, routing platforms (Leandata), or forecasting tools (Clari).
Ability to partner effectively with Business systems and analytics teams.

scope.surface: Partner-led and geographically distributed revenue motions — pipeline management, forecasting, channel attribution, international coverage
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS, AI-native business communications platform
core_responsibilities: Define and enforce opportunity stage entry and exit criteria
Establish deal inspection frameworks by segment and deal size
Identify and eliminate poorly qualified pipeline, stalled deals, and inflated forecasts
Own forecast methodology and call discipline across segments
Lead or co-lead weekly forecast calls with Sales leadership
Track and surface forecast bias, slippage patterns, and risk signals
Design and run pipeline councils by segment
Translate execution issues into process improvements and system enforcement
Define and own core pipeline and forecast KPIs
Monitor partner-sourced pipeline integrity and attribution
Support forecast validation for partner-led deals
Monitor territory and partner coverage sufficiency

green_flags: Clear reporting structure to VP of Revenue Operations
Role is explicitly described as revenue execution, not reporting support — signals real ownership
Specific tooling stack listed (Salesforce, Tableau, Outreach, Clay, Scratchpad, ZoomInfo, Clari, Leandata)
Encourages candidates who don't meet every qualification to apply
Established company with 70,000+ customers and named enterprise logos
Two office options listed (Austin, TX and Tempe, AZ)
red_flags: Office time requirement for hybrid not specified
'SaaS experience strongly preferred' is listed under must-haves section but uses preferred language — classification ambiguity

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years range and reporting structure)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false — no direct reports mentioned)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicit KPIs listed)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as false — JD says 'based in our Austin or Tempe offices')
compensation.equity_mentioned (inferred as false — not stated in JD)
ambiguities: 'SaaS experience strongly preferred' appears under the skills section alongside hard requirements. 'Strongly preferred' language suggests soft, but placement with unconditional bullets creates ambiguity. Classified as soft (nice_to_have).
Location type: JD states 'opportunity to be based in our Austin or Tempe offices' — implies office-based but does not specify required days in office. Classified as hybrid with unclear office time.

'Ability to partner effectively with Business systems and analytics teams' is listed under the same skills section as hard requirements but reads as a collaborative capability rather than a strict gate; classified as soft.
